title: Fuel-Corrected Causal Degradation Slopes
summary: F1 lap times mix tyre degradation with fuel burn-off, traffic, and abnormal laps.
rationale: F1 lap times mix tyre degradation with fuel burn-off, traffic, and abnormal laps. A causal residual degradation slope within each driver stint may identify tyres approaching the performance cliff better than static TyreLife and Cumulative_Degradation features.
implementation_hint: For each Race_Year, estimate a robust race-lap baseline pace from non-pit laps, then compute driver-stint residual pace. Add past-only rolling means, slopes, curvature, last-lap z-scores, and residual acceleration over windows like 2, 3, and 5 laps, grouped by Race_Year, Driver, Stint.
expected_effect: Can lift AUC by ranking imminent stops higher when pace is deteriorating quickly even if absolute tyre age is not extreme.
risk: Neutralized, wet, or traffic laps can contaminate slopes; features must be strictly causal within folds and not use future laps.
